title: Idempotency, Inbox Transactions, and Effects · Go Build Room
summary: A Go pairing room incrementally implements a duplicate-safe Go effect state machine, first with deterministic values and then with a changed failure, while keeping prior messaging invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a duplicate-safe Go effect state machine while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Derive a stable scoped operation identity from producer intent, tenant, message type, and semantic version rather than delivery metadata; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Record inbox identity, input fingerprint, state transition, domain effect, and completion atomically in one database transaction where possible; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Reject reuse of an operation identity with a different payload fingerprint or semantic intent and retain conflict evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Stable Idempotency Key
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go messaging team handles case 4065 during canary Go consumer decoding old and new envelopes. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject fingerprint conflicts, suppress completed duplicates, and preserve external ambiguity; retain rollback topology before deleting old bindings; then exhaust retry age before attempts reach their maximum. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Derive a stable scoped operation identity from producer intent, tenant, message type, and semantic version rather than delivery metadata; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Stable Idempotency Key.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for derive a stable scoped operation identity from producer intent, tenant, message type, and semantic version rather than delivery metadata; in go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence. For RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Stable Idempotency Key, keep Stable Idempotency Key connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

For commit to the mission model, run one convenient Stable Idempotency Key happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output.
